chapter_title.en: Chapter 84 – The striving regarding a need of the Momin

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (112/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (112/ 9)
text: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) مَنْ مَشَى فِي حَاجَةِ أَخِيهِ الْمُسْلِمِ أَظَلَّهُ اللَّهُ بِخَمْسَةٍ وَ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ مَلَكٍ وَ لَمْ يَرْفَعْ قَدَماً إِلَّا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ حَسَنَةً وَ حَطَّ عَنْهُ بِهَا سَيِّئَةً وَ يَرْفَعُ لَهُ بِهَا دَرَجَةً فَإِذَا فَرَغَ مِنْ حَاجَتِهِ كَتَبَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ بِهَا أَجْرَ حَاجٍّ وَ مُعْتَمِرٍ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ahmad Bin Usman Bin Isa, from a man, from Abu Ubeyda Al Haza’a who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who walks regarding a need of his Muslim brother, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Shade him by seventy thousand Angels, and he would not raise a foot except that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Write a good deed for him, and drop a sin from him due to it, and Elevate a level for him due to it. So when he is free from his need, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Write for him, due to it, the Recompense of a performer of a Hajj and an Umrah’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from Ahmad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from a man from abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’ who abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far (a.s.) has said, ‘One who walks to help his Muslim brother (in belief) Allah will make seventy-five thousand angels to provide him shadow and for each step that he walks Allah writes for him one good deed, deletes one of his sins and raises him by one degree in meritorious satus. When he completes the help, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, writes for him the reward for one Hajj and one ‘Umrah.’”
